_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-13 SPRN Transport Procedure (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-13: SPRN Transport Procedure — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define the step-by-step transport procedure for SPRN patient transport including patient belongings, documentation, travel planning, and destination arrival protocols.

sections.title: Pre-Transport Coordination
content: Contact MDHHS SPRN coordinator before accepting patient — receive patient classification (Protocol 10-11) and destination (Protocol 10-10)
Confirm receiving hospital SPRN readiness — receiving team must be in PPE before EMS arrives
Confirm travel plan with MDHHS coordinator — MDHHS coordinates route for long-distance transports
Brief all crew on patient classification, route, hospital destination, and emergency contact numbers
Complete pre-transport supply checklist (Protocol 10-12)
Don PPE before departure to patient location

sections.title: Patient Belongings
content: All patient belongings (clothing, personal items, electronics) must be handled as potentially contaminated
Place all belongings in BIOHAZARD bags — use two bags (double-bag)
Label bags: patient name, date, 'BIOHAZARD — POTENTIAL HCID EXPOSURE'
DO NOT allow patient to handle unbagged personal items during transport
Belongings accompany patient to receiving facility — handled per hospital HCID procedures
Cell phones and electronics: if patient must use phone during transport, wipe with disinfectant after use; place in biohazard bag

sections.title: Travel Plan — MDHHS Coordination
content: For transports >2 hours: MDHHS coordinates a formal travel plan including:
— Route selection (major roads preferred — minimizes stops/detours)
— Patient Transfer Points (PTPs): pre-identified locations at approximately 2-hour intervals where:
* Team rotation occurs (care provider team swap if multi-hour transport)
* Crew can take breaks and doff/re-don PPE safely
* Vehicle fuel stop if needed
— Law enforcement escort coordination if required
— Emergency diversion hospitals along route
For transports <2 hours: informal travel plan; MDHHS coordinator advises if stops needed
Do NOT deviate from MDHHS-coordinated route without notification — contact SPRN coordinator if route change needed

sections.title: Documentation During Transport
content: All documentation must occur AFTER transport is complete if possible — minimize paper/equipment contamination in patient care area
If documentation required during transport: use disposable forms or tablet that can be decontaminated
Written documentation completed after doff at receiving facility
PCR: identify as SPRN transport; note patient type, PPE used, interventions, and transport time
MDHHS SPRN incident number must be recorded on all documentation
Any contaminated paper or electronic devices: treat as Category A biohazard waste

sections.title: Destination Arrival — Receiving Hospital
content: Notify receiving hospital 10-15 minutes before arrival — confirm receiving team is in position
Approach designated SPRN ambulance entrance (pre-coordinated with hospital) — do NOT use standard EMS entrance
Patient handoff to hospital SPRN receiving team:
— Verbal report including patient type, interventions en route, and clinical status
— Transfer patient from ambulance stretcher to hospital team in designated isolation area
— Transfer of patient belongings (bagged biohazard bags)
— Transfer of patient care documentation
After patient handoff: EMS crew remains in designated doffing area at hospital
Doff PPE with hospital safety officer assistance per Protocol 10-17 doffing sequence
Vehicle remains in designated contaminated vehicle area until decontaminated — Protocol 10-15
Take post-transport vital signs per Protocol 10-17 (team member health monitoring)

sections.title: Decommissioning Contaminated Materials
content: All supplies used during SPRN transport are considered potentially contaminated
Dispose of: single-use PPE components, used medical supplies, contaminated forms — Category A biohazard waste
Reusable equipment (PAPR, MITD components): decontaminate per manufacturer specifications and Protocol 10-15
Vehicle: do NOT return to service until decontamination per Protocol 10-15 is complete
Timeline: ambulance decontamination must be completed before vehicle returns to service — coordinate with hospital or MDHHS decon team

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: Patient clinical deterioration during transport — contact SPRN coordinator and online MC; consider diversion to nearest SPRN-capable facility; life support decisions per Protocol 10-14
PPE breach during transport — alert partner; do NOT panic; contact SPRN coordinator; document breach; post-transport health monitoring Protocol 10-17
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. MDHHS coordinates the travel plan for SPRN transports — EMS does not independently plan routing. Patient Transfer Points (PTPs) enable team rotation and safe crew breaks on long transports. All contaminated materials are Category A biohazard waste. Ambulance is out of service until decontaminated per Protocol 10-15.
